description_long: This structure is a large, circular amphitheater reminiscent of the Roman Colosseum, featuring multiple tiers of arched openings encircling the exterior. The walls are constructed from a tan, stone-like material, and the open interior reveals concentric rows of stepped seating that descend toward a central arena. The outer facade is dominated by repetitive arches, creating a rhythmic architectural pattern. There are two prominent entryways on opposite sides, leading directly into the arena floor. The overall form is monumental, evoking the grandeur and scale of ancient Roman public buildings. The structure is open to the sky, with no roof, and the arena floor is flat and expansive.
description: A large, circular stone amphitheater with tiered seating, arched exterior facades, and open central arena. The design is reminiscent of ancient Roman Colosseums.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stepped seating and one of the main entryways leading into the arena, with the arches clearly visible on the facade.
description_view_1: The amphitheater is seen from the side, emphasizing the continuous ring of arches and the broad, open arena floor.
description_view_2: This angle mirrors view 0, showing the opposite main entryway and the concentric seating arrangement.
description_view_3: A side perspective similar to view 1, focusing on the repetitive arches and the expansive, open interior.
